content:
You are an autonomous agent working to fulfill the user's goals.
Avoid rewriting files, prefer incremental edits.
Always add the test case to the test suite before implementing it.
Avoid using exception messages, use error codes.
Always prefer the simplest solution possible.

[Session context summary — replaces earlier history]
The user's goal was to fix two failing tests related to block scoping in a Tuff compiler project, then resolve subsequent linting and code duplication issues without changing project configuration. The root cause of the test failures (`let mut x = 0; { x = 1; } x` returning `immutable_reassignment`, and `{ let x = 0; } x` not detecting an undeclared variable) was in the segment-splitting loop at line ~261: when encountering a closing brace that returned `braceDepth` to 0, the code pushed `currentSegment` without first appending the `}` character. This meant block detection (`trimmed.startsWith("{") && trimmed.endsWith("}")`) always failed since segments like `{ x = 1;` lacked their trailing brace. The fix was adding `currentSegment += ch;` before the depth-0 check, so blocks are properly recognized and inner-scope variables don't leak outward while outer-scope mutable vars remain accessible inside blocks.

After that fix, TypeScript flagged possibly-undefined string index accesses on lines ~460 and ~468 (`c2`, `firstCh`). These were resolved with non-null assertions (`!`) since the loop bounds guarantee valid indices. Then PMD CPD detected two duplications: a 39-line let-declaration parsing/emission block repeated between inner-block processing (line ~306) and outer-scope processing (line ~389), plus a 14-line boolean-aware return emission pattern at lines ~363 and ~479. Both were extracted into helper functions: `processLetDecl(restOfDeclInput)` handles mut detection, variable name/type extraction, duplicate checking, scope registration, and assignment emission; `emitReturn(retExpr)` centralizes the bool-literal/bool-type/logical-operator/comparison-expression check that wraps returns in `(+(...))`. The incremental edits caused some stray braces/duplicate lines that required cleanup.

All 23 tests pass, TypeScript compiles cleanly with no errors, ESLint passes, and CPD reports zero duplications above the 50-token threshold. No open tasks remain.
Got it, I have the prior context.
